easy.q: Who was the first President of the United States?
Which president's face is on the quarter?
Which president signed the Emancipation Proclamation in 1863?
Who was known by the nickname 'Honest Abe'?
Who was president during most of World War II?
Which president served four terms — more than any other?
Which president resigned from office amid the Watergate scandal?
Who established the Environmental Protection Agency in 1970?
Who was the youngest person ever elected president of the United States?
Which president said 'Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country'?
Who was the first African American elected president of the United States?
Which president won the Nobel Peace Prize in 2009?
Who wrote the Declaration of Independence and later served as the third president?
Which president purchased the Louisiana Territory from France in 1803?
Who signed the Civil Rights Act of 1964?
Which president oversaw the Great Society programs, including Medicare and Medicaid?
Who ordered the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945?
Which president was commander-in-chief during the Korean War?
Who survived an assassination attempt in Washington, D.C., in March 1981?
Which president said 'Tear down this wall!' in a 1987 speech at the Berlin Wall?
Who was president when the United States entered World War I in 1917?
Which president was in office when the Panama Canal opened in August 1914?
Who was a peanut farmer from Plains, Georgia, before becoming president?
Which president negotiated the Camp David Accords between Egypt and Israel in 1978?
Which president was in office when the Berlin Wall fell in 1989?
Who was commander-in-chief during the first Gulf War in 1991?
Which president was in office during the September 11, 2001 attacks?
Who won the 2000 presidential election after the Supreme Court halted Florida's recount?
Who created NASA with the National Aeronautics and Space Act of 1958?
Which president was in office when Alaska and Hawaii became states in 1959?
Who won the presidential election of 2016?
Which president was impeached twice — in 2019 and again in 2021?
Which president took office at age 78 after defeating incumbent Donald Trump in the 2020 election?
Which president won the 2020 election?
Who signed NAFTA into law in 1993?
Which president was impeached by the House in 1998 but acquitted by the Senate?
Who was the second president of the United States?
Which president was the first to live in the White House?
Who was known as the 'Father of the Constitution'?
Which president was nicknamed 'Old Hickory' and won the Battle of New Orleans?
Who was the first vice president to succeed a president who died in office?
Which president's face is carved into Mount Rushmore alongside Washington, Jefferson, and Lincoln?
Who was the only U.S. president to serve two non-consecutive terms?
Who was president when the nation celebrated its bicentennial in 1976?
Whose name inspired the 'teddy bear' toy, after he refused to shoot a tied-up bear while hunting?
Which president was the 22nd and 24th — the only person to serve two non-consecutive terms?
Who became president when Richard Nixon resigned in August 1974?
Which president's two terms from 1817 to 1825 were called the 'Era of Good Feelings'?
Which president succeeded William Henry Harrison after 31 days and was expelled from the Whig Party?
Who was president during the Stock Market Crash of 1929 at the start of the Great Depression?
Which president died in office from illness in August 1923, before scandals in his administration were exposed?
Who was shot and killed at a Washington train station six months into his presidency in 1881?
Which president was assassinated in Buffalo, New York, in September 1901?
Which president was born in Kinderhook, New York, and was the first president born a U.S. citizen?
Who was known as 'Old Rough and Ready' and served as president from 1849 to 1850 before dying in office?

medium.q: Who was the only president to hold a patent, for a device to lift boats over shallow water?
Which president signed the Federal Aid Highway Act of 1956, creating the Interstate Highway System?
Who was the only president to serve as Chief Justice of the Supreme Court after his presidency?
Which president was the first to throw out the ceremonial first pitch at a Major League Baseball game?
Who promoted the 'Square Deal' domestic agenda focused on labor rights and curbing corporate power?
Which president set aside more than 230 million acres for national parks, forests, and monuments?
Who was the first president to be born west of the Mississippi River, in West Branch, Iowa?
Who was president when the U.S. stock market crashed on Black Tuesday in October 1929?
Who was the only bachelor president, having never married?
Which campaign used the slogan 'Tippecanoe and Tyler Too' in 1840?
Which president served the shortest term, dying of pneumonia 31 days after his inauguration?
Which president issued the Monroe Doctrine, warning European powers against colonizing the Americas?
Who signed the Sherman Antitrust Act of 1890?
Which president was the first to be born a U.S. citizen rather than a British colonial subject?
Who was a film and television actor before being elected governor of California and then president?
Which president's 1984 campaign produced the iconic 'It's Morning Again in America' advertisement?
Who won the disputed election of 1800 after a House runoff against Aaron Burr?
Who was president during the Teapot Dome oil bribery scandal?
Which president signed the Americans with Disabilities Act in 1990?
Who ran against Ronald Reagan in the 1984 presidential election?
Who was the only president elected unanimously by the Electoral College?
Which president coined the phrase 'the only thing we have to fear is fear itself' in his 1933 inaugural address?
Who signed the GI Bill of Rights in 1944, giving veterans access to education and housing loans?
Which president's nickname was 'Ike'?
Which president was the first to debate his opponent on live national television, in September 1960?
Who was the only president to earn a Purple Heart, wounded in PT-boat action in the Pacific?
Which president kept a sign reading 'The buck stops here' on his Oval Office desk?
Who was president when the United States recognized the State of Israel in May 1948?
Who was the only president to earn a Ph.D., from Johns Hopkins University?
Which president established the modern precedent of delivering the State of the Union in person before Congress?
Who ran as a major independent candidate in 1992, winning about 19 percent of the popular vote?
Which president voluntarily stepped down after a single term, as he had pledged during his 1844 campaign?
Who was the last president who had also previously served as Speaker of the House of Representatives?
Who oversaw Reconstruction after the Civil War and was later impeached over the Tenure of Office Act?
Which president ran against and narrowly defeated incumbent George H.W. Bush in 1992?
Who ran against George W. Bush in the 2004 presidential election?
Who succeeded to the presidency when Warren Harding died in office in August 1923?
Which president was the last Whig ever elected to the presidency, winning the 1848 election?
Who was president when the last U.S. combat troops were withdrawn from Vietnam under the Paris Peace Accords?
Which president launched the 'War on Drugs' as a formal domestic policy priority in 1971?
Which president signed the Immigration Act of 1965, abolishing national-origin immigration quotas?
Who won the 1976 election as a Washington outsider, largely unknown nationally a year before the primaries?
Who was the last president to be born in a log cabin, born in Moreland Hills, Ohio, in 1831?
Who signed the Homestead Act of 1862, granting settlers 160 acres of public land?
Who was president when the United States purchased the Gadsden strip from Mexico in 1853?
Which president's nickname was 'Old Rough and Ready,' earned during the Mexican-American War?
Who was the only president never elected to either the presidency or the vice presidency?
Which president signed the Affordable Care Act into law in March 2010?
Who signed the USA PATRIOT Act in October 2001, greatly expanding government surveillance powers?
Which president signed the Missouri Compromise of 1820, temporarily settling slavery in new territories?
Who was the first president to be tried for impeachment and acquitted by the Senate, in 1868?
Which president won the 2008 election, becoming the first African American to win a major-party presidential nomination?
Who signed the Civil Rights Act of 1968, also known as the Fair Housing Act, in his final months in office?
Which president was impeached by the House in December 2019 but acquitted by the Senate in February 2020?
Who was the only president to serve in Congress after his presidency, dying at his desk on the House floor in 1848?

hard.q: Which president was born on July 4th — the only president born on Independence Day?
Which president signed the Pendleton Civil Service Reform Act of 1883, creating a merit-based federal employment system?
Who was the only president to serve between Grover Cleveland's two non-consecutive terms?
Which presidential campaign used the slogan 'He Kept Us Out of War' in 1916?
Which president signed the Morrill Land-Grant Act of 1862, funding the creation of public agricultural colleges?
Who was the only son of a former president also elected to the presidency, before the Bush family?
Which president negotiated the Treaty of Portsmouth in 1905, earning a Nobel Peace Prize for ending the Russo-Japanese War?
Who was president when the 17th Amendment providing for direct popular election of senators was ratified in 1913?
Which president oversaw the annexation of Texas just before leaving office in March 1845?
Who was the first president to appoint an African American to the Cabinet, naming Robert C. Weaver HUD Secretary in 1966?
Which president delivered the longest inaugural address in U.S. history, speaking for nearly two hours in freezing weather, and died one month later?
Which president signed the Dawes Act of 1887, dividing Native American tribal lands into individual allotments?
Which president's first lady was nicknamed 'Lemonade Lucy' for banning alcohol from White House events?
Who was the first president to visit all 50 states while in office?
Which president won the disputed 1876 election despite losing the popular vote, after a congressional commission ruled in his favor?
Who was the first president to ride in a submarine, descending in the USS Plunger off the Connecticut coast in 1905?
Which president signed the Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act of 1930, sharply raising import duties on thousands of goods?
Who was the last Whig Party president, assuming office in 1850 after Zachary Taylor died?
Who was president when the U.S. acquired Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines after the Spanish-American War in 1898?
Which presidential campaign used the slogan 'Return to Normalcy' in 1920?
Who was the first president to have his voice preserved on audio recording, captured on a wax cylinder?
Who was the only former president elected to the House of Representatives after leaving the White House, serving 17 years in Congress?
Who was the first president to appoint a woman to the Supreme Court, naming Sandra Day O'Connor in 1981?
Which president signed the Missouri Compromise of 1820, temporarily balancing slave and free states?
Who is said to be the only president to receive a speeding citation while in office, for riding his horse too fast through Washington streets?
Which president delivered the farewell address warning of the dangers of a 'military-industrial complex' in January 1961?
Which president issued Executive Order 9981 in 1948, desegregating the U.S. armed forces?
Who was the only president to pardon a predecessor, granting Richard Nixon a full pardon in September 1974?
Which president established the two-term precedent by voluntarily declining to seek a third term in 1796?
Which president negotiated the Gadsden Purchase of 1853, adding territory that became southern Arizona and New Mexico?
Who signed the Compromise of 1850 piece by piece after assuming office, temporarily averting a secession crisis?
Which president created the Children's Bureau in 1912, the first federal agency dedicated to child welfare?
Who was the first president to hold an online town hall, via AOL in 1999?
Which president signed the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo in 1848, ending the Mexican-American War and adding the Southwest to the United States?
Who ran the famous 'whistlestop' railroad campaign of 1948, defying polls that predicted his defeat?
Which president signed the Kansas-Nebraska Act of 1854, repealing the Missouri Compromise and reigniting conflict over slavery?
Who was president when the United States acquired Alaska from Russia in 1867?
Which president's administration coined the term 'détente' to describe its strategy of easing Cold War tensions?
Who was the first president to be impeached by the House, in 1868, after dismissing his Secretary of War?
Which president signed the Indian Removal Act of 1830, forcing the Five Civilized Tribes off their ancestral lands?
Who was the first president born in a hospital, in Plains, Georgia, in 1924?
Which president championed the Open Door Policy toward China in 1899, seeking equal trading rights for all nations?
Who was the first president to appoint an openly gay Cabinet secretary, naming Pete Buttigieg as Transportation Secretary in 2021?
Which president signed the Reciprocal Trade Agreements Act of 1934, shifting tariff authority from Congress to the executive branch?
Who was president when American Marines were sent to Lebanon during the 1958 political crisis?
Who was the first president to be born east of the Alleghenies outside of Virginia or Massachusetts, born in the Waxhaws region in 1767?
Which president signed the Lend-Lease Act of 1941, providing war materials to Allied nations before the U.S. entered the war?
Who issued the first presidential pardon, granted to leaders of the Whiskey Rebellion in 1795?
Which president was the first to campaign successfully using radio broadcasts in 1924?
Who signed the Voting Rights Act of 1965 and announced he would not seek reelection in March 1968?
Which president appointed the first Hispanic Cabinet secretary, naming Lauro Cavazos as Education Secretary in 1988?
Who was the only president to win election while serving as a sitting member of the House of Representatives?
Which president signed the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977, agreeing to return the canal to Panamanian control?
Which president signed the Sherman Silver Purchase Act of 1890, and later fought to repeal it during a financial crisis?
Which president negotiated the Webster-Ashburton Treaty of 1842, settling the U.S.-Canadian border in the northeast?
